name: TensorFlow Multiworker On Slurm
summary: Plan and smoke test TensorFlow MultiWorkerMirroredStrategy jobs on Slurm.
description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm pattern for reviewing TensorFlow multi-worker launch settings, including TF_CONFIG generation, worker addresses, task indexes, GPU visibility, strategy initialization, and a tiny distributed tensor reduction before running real training.

inputs.name: gpu_partition
inputs.description: Slurm partition or queue that provides GPU nodes.

inputs.name: tensorflow_environment
inputs.description: Module, virtual environment, Conda environment, or container that provides TensorFlow for the target CPU or GPU backend.

inputs.name: resources
inputs.description: Slurm nodes, tasks per node, GPUs per node, CPUs, memory, and wall time requested for the TensorFlow smoke test.

inputs.name: worker_port
inputs.description: TCP port used in each TensorFlow worker address inside TF_CONFIG.

outputs.name: tensorflow_smoke_log
outputs.description: Slurm log containing TensorFlow version, TF_CONFIG, device visibility, strategy metadata, and smoke-test status.

outputs.name: launch_record
outputs.description: Reviewable launch plan with selected worker addresses, task count, worker port, and dry-run or execution mode.

examples.title: Plan or run a TensorFlow multi-worker Slurm smoke test

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check the Slurm wrapper shell syntax.
Check the Python smoke script syntax without requiring TensorFlow to be installed.

Replace account, partition, and environment placeholders, submit with RUN_TENSORFLOW_SMOKE=0 to review the launch plan, then submit with RUN_TENSORFLOW_SMOKE=1 on a short GPU allocation and confirm every worker reports status=ok.
